skills.name: Basic Elements
skills.desc: Basic HTML elements such as <html>, <head>, <title>, and <body> are essential components of any HTML document, providing the overall structure and content.

skills.name: Head Elements
skills.desc: Head elements like <meta>, <link>, and <script> are used to define metadata, link external resources, and include scripts in the document's head section.

skills.name: Character Encoding
skills.desc: Character encoding, specified with <meta charset>, determines how characters are represented in the document, ensuring proper text rendering.

skills.name: Language Declaration
skills.desc: The <html> tag's 'lang' attribute declares the language of the document, aiding in accessibility and proper rendering for different languages.

skills.name: Viewport Declaration
skills.desc: Viewport declarations using the <meta viewport> tag help control how the web page is displayed on various devices, enhancing responsiveness.

skills.name: Text and Headings
skills.desc:
HTML provides tags like

,

to

, and for organizing and formatting text, including headings of various levels.

skills.name: Paragraphs and Line Breaks
skills.desc:
The

tag is used for paragraphs, while
is used for line breaks to control the layout and presentation of text.

skills.name: Lists and Descriptions
skills.desc: HTML supports ordered (<ol>) and unordered (<ul>) lists, as well as definition lists (<dl>) for organizing content.

skills.name: Quotations
skills.desc: Quotations can be marked up using <blockquote> and <q> tags, providing semantic meaning to quoted content.

skills.name: Text Direction
skills.desc: The 'dir' attribute and <bdo> tag enable control over the text direction, useful for multilingual content.

skills.name: Layout Elements
skills.desc: Layout elements such as <header>, <footer>, <nav>, and <main> help structure and organize the layout of web pages.

skills.name: Navigation and Links
skills.desc: Navigation elements like <a> (anchor) tags are used to create links within and between web pages, aiding in user navigation.

skills.name: Document Flow Understanding
skills.desc: Understanding the natural document flow and display properties of HTML elements is essential for creating cohesive and responsive layouts.

skills.name: Accessibility Considerations
skills.desc: Considering accessibility throughout the HTML structure, including landmarks like <header> and ARIA attributes, ensures inclusivity for all users.

skills.name: Multimedia Elements
skills.desc: Multimedia elements, such as , allow the inclusion of images, audio, and video content within web pages.

skills.name: Image Maps
skills.desc: Image maps created with <map> and <area> tags enable clickable regions on an image, often used for navigation.

skills.name: Figure and Caption Association
skills.desc: The <figure> and <figcaption> elements are used to associate captions with images or other multimedia content for better accessibility and semantics.

skills.name: Embedding External Media
skills.desc: External media content like videos from YouTube or audio clips can be embedded using <iframe> and <audio> or <video> tags.

skills.name: Table Structure
skills.desc: Tables are created using <table>, <tr>, <th>, and <td> elements to organize data into rows and columns for tabular representation.

skills.name: Complex Tables
skills.desc: Complex tables involve spanning cells, headers, and captions, offering advanced structuring and presentation options.

skills.name: Table Accessibility
skills.desc: Ensuring tables are accessible with appropriate headers and markup is vital for assistive technologies and users with disabilities.

skills.name: Form Elements
skills.desc: HTML provides a range of form elements like <input>, <textarea>, and <select> for gathering user input and submitting data.

skills.name: Form Validation
skills.desc: Form validation using attributes like 'required' and JavaScript enables client-side validation, enhancing data quality.

skills.name: Grouping Form Elements
skills.desc: Grouping form elements within <fieldset> and <legend> tags enhances form organization and accessibility.

skills.name: Accessibility in Forms
skills.desc: Ensuring forms are accessible with proper labels and ARIA attributes is crucial for users with disabilities to navigate and use forms.

skills.name: Interactive and Dynamic Elements
skills.desc: Interactive and dynamic elements, such as <iframe>, <audio>, <video>, and <canvas>, enable the creation of engaging and interactive web content that responds to user actions.

skills.name: Custom Data Attributes
skills.desc: Custom data attributes (data-* attributes) provide a way to store custom data for JavaScript or CSS use without affecting HTML semantics.

skills.name: HTML Templating
skills.desc: HTML templates, often used with <template> tags, allow developers to define reusable content that can be cloned and inserted dynamically with JavaScript.
